description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a polished, glossy surface featuring a striking black base color interlaced with white and reddish-brown veining, reminiscent of high-end marble such as Nero Portoro. The block has sharp, well-defined edges and flat faces, suggesting it was precisely cut and finished for architectural or decorative use. The veining patterns are irregular and organic, running across all visible faces, giving the block a luxurious and natural appearance. The surface reflects light, indicating a high degree of polish, and there are no visible chips, cracks, or signs of significant wear. The block appears solid and dense, likely composed entirely of natural stone. Its size and finish suggest it could serve as a barrier, bench, or decorative element in upscale urban environments.
description: A polished, rectangular black marble block with dramatic white and reddish veining, featuring sharp edges and a glossy finish.
description_view_0: Front view shows the broad, flat face with prominent white and reddish veining on a black background.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the block's narrow width and the continuation of the veining pattern across the side face.
description_view_2: Top view highlights the largest surface area, displaying the most intricate and dense veining patterns.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors the first side, with visible veining and a slightly different distribution of colors.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a decorative barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set on a level concrete or stone base
attribute_weathering_pattern: no visible weathering; surface appears freshly polished and well-maintained
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
